X1.reason: Unsupported or contradictory numeric claim appears.

X2.reason: Estimated or lower-confidence values are hedged.

X3.reason: Uses a population-style claim as if it explains the user specifically.

X4.reason: Uses no more than one follow-up question.

X5.reason: No unrequested device or app brands are named.

X6.reason: Shape or length misses the rubric window (95 words).

X7.reason: Red-flag presentation is not handled with safety-first triage.

T1.reason: Does not lead by acknowledging the concerning symptom.

T2.reason: Stops coaching instead of prescribing training.

T3.reason: Understates the urgency of care needed.

T4.reason: Does not use wearable metrics as reassurance.

G1.reason: Does not frame progress against the stated target metric or band.

G2.reason: Missing a soft time-bounded next milestone or focus.

G3.reason: Uses progress-oriented framing rather than alarm framing.
